title: Isopes Fabules
summary: CME00105 is a repository copy of the University of Michigan encoding of selected pages from Julius Zupitza's 1890 article 'Zu Lydgates Isopus.' The XML encodes Fables I-IV from Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.19 and Fable VII from Bodleian MS Ashmole 59; the latter reached Zupitza through a working transcription supplied by Arthur Sampson Napier. Zupitza collated corresponding readings from British Library Harley MS 2251. The graph keeps the one seven-fable abstract work, five-fable selection, three witnesses, mediated transcript, edition, encoding, repository artifact, facsimiles, catalogs, and later edition distinct.
review_notes: DIMEV record 6701 supports one seven-fable abstract-work boundary; CME00105 is an edited selection of I-IV and VII, not five unrelated abstract works.
DIMEV's Harley source note appears inconsistent with its full Harley locus and current BL and Wheatley descriptions; this manifest follows current catalog evidence while preserving the discrepancy.
Complete means the complete provider-published digital object or source file, not necessarily every physical manuscript surface or a preservation master.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00105
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow and supplied through the pinned Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English item CME00105
entities.description: Electronic encoding of Zupitza's printed pp. 6-25, beginning partway through printed p. 6 and ending before the notes section on p. 25. It preserves Fables I-IV and VII, German prefatory prose for Fable VII, notes, typography, printed pagination, and manuscript-folio milestones while omitting printed pp. 1-5, the opening prose on p. 6, Fables V-VI, and the remainder of pp. 25-28.

entities.label: Julius Zupitza, Zu Lydgates Isopus (1890)
entities.description: A twenty-eight-page article. Section I reports Zupitza's direct inspection of Harley 2251; section II prints the Trinity texts and collates Harley readings; section III prints Ashmole Fable VII from a transcription supplied by Napier and collates Harley; section IV supplies further London-only notes. Zupitza normalizes capitalization and some word division, marks expansions in italics, suppresses certain manuscript strokes, and presents substantive London variants in footnotes.

bibliographic.citation: Julius Zupitza, 'Zu Lydgates Isopus,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen 85, XLIV. Jahrgang (Braunschweig: George Westermann, 1890), 1-28.
bibliographic.title: Zu Lydgates Isopus
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.19
entities.description: A paper anthology of English poems by John Lydgate and others, produced across the fifteenth and sixteenth centuries. Fables I-IV occupy ff. 12ra-16ra; the fourth stops after three stanzas and most of f. 16 is blank. Later John Stow leaves at ff. 235-237 contain two other fables but are not direct sources for CME00105.

holding.notes: Owned by John Stow and given by George Wilmer.

notes: Fables I-IV are ff. 12ra-16ra.
The separately copied Stow fables at ff. 235-237 are outside CME00105's direct-source boundary.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Ashmole 59
entities.description: A composite paper codex of three separately produced units. Part I, ff. 1-133, is in John Shirley's hand and dated 1447-1456. Item 7 at ff. 24v-25r contains only the four stanzas of Fable VII, with a rubric naming John Lydgate and Oxford.

holding.notes: Ashmole bequest; transferred to the Bodleian in 1860.

physical_description.support: Paper, with fifteenth-century parchment flyleaves and modern endleaves
physical_description.extent: Composite: x modern paper endleaves + ii fifteenth-century parchment flyleaves + 134 fifteenth-century paper leaves + 50 sixteenth-century paper leaves + ix modern endleaves
physical_description.format: Composite codex of three codicological units
notes: Late-seventeenth-century binding.
Target Fable VII is imperfect and consists of four stanzas at ff. 24v-25r.

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 2251
entities.description: A London paper manuscript dated 1460-1483, copied by the Hammond Scribe, perhaps John Multon, apparently for John Vale. It is the only surviving medieval witness containing all seven Isopes Fabules, at ff. 257r-270v, though Wheatley notes a missing leaf corresponding to lines 267-322.

holding.notes: The Hammond Scribe and John Multon identifications and commission for John Vale remain qualified catalog attributions.

physical_description.extent: 293 folios, three unfoliated paper flyleaves at each end, and one unfoliated leaf after f. 178
notes: Isopes Fabules occupies ff. 257r-270v.

entities.label: Arthur Sampson Napier's working transcription of Ashmole 59 Fable VII
entities.description: A historically attested but presently unlocated working transcription of MS. Ashmole 59, ff. 24v-25r. Zupitza states that he owed the transcription to his friend Napier. Same-volume and authority evidence strongly identifies him as Arthur Sampson Napier; the transcript's format, date, present location, and survival are unknown.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Archiv volume 85 (1890)
entities.description: The complete 502-page Robarts Library/University of Toronto scan, digitized in 2008 at 400 ppi. Zupitza's article is printed pp. 1-28, PDF pp. 17-44; CME00105 draws from printed pp. 6-25.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete volume PDF, OCR text, and item metadata

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge IIIF facsimile of R.3.19
entities.description: The complete provider-published IIIF Presentation 2 sequence of 522 canvases. Fables I-IV map to canvases 29-37 (viewer indices 28-36), images/000029.jpg-images/000037.jpg in the retained 800-pixel bundle. Complete here means the entire provider manifest sequence, not independently verified preservation-master coverage of every physical surface.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and color page images

entities.label: DIMEV v1.0 record 6701
entities.description: The full Digital Index of Middle English Verse record for John Lydgate's seven-fable Isopes Fabules, IMEV/NIMEV 4178, with incipit, form, subjects, witnesses, loci, editions, and bibliography.

entities.label: Edward Wheatley, Isopes Fabules (2013)
entities.description: A later complete Middle English Texts Series edition used as supporting scholarship for work boundary, date, source, thematic, and witness claims. It is not an upstream source of the 1890-derived U-M encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Edward Wheatley, ed., Isopes Fabules, Middle English Texts Series (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 2013).

access.access_method: Ask first whether the legacy digitization can be supplied. Otherwise request covers, spine if offered, pastedowns, all six unfoliated flyleaves, all 293 folios recto and verso, the unfoliated leaf after f. 178, blanks, stubs, cancelled leaves, repairs, and edges if offered; identify ff. 257r-270v separately.

rights.component: Fifteenth-century Middle English text represented in the XML, excluding modern editorial matter, encoding, and images
rights.basis: The medieval text and surviving fifteenth-century witness copies predate modern copyright terms; this does not decide rights in modern editions, markup, scans, photographs, database content, or provider terms.

rights.component: U-M electronic transcription, selection, structure, and markup
rights.basis: No item-level reuse statement was inspectable during the current Cloudflare challenge. Rights in the medieval text do not settle rights in modern encoding and markup.
The article was published in 1890; current U.S. duration guidance places such publication outside copyright.

rights.component: Internet Archive/Robarts scan derivatives and item metadata
rights.basis: Exact item metadata asserts possible status NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT for the United States based on the 1890 date and no visible notice. Internet Archive provides no explicit license or warranty for this item; the assertion does not establish worldwide status.

rights.component: Trinity R.3.19 IIIF manifest and page images
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest supplies Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0. Trinity's general Wren Digital Collections guidance also asks users to seek permission for publication; preserve both layers and confirm intended reuse with the provider.

rights.component: Prospective Bodleian-supplied reference scans of out-of-copyright material
rights.basis: Current mediated-copying guidance says supplied scans of out-of-copyright materials use CC BY-NC 4.0 and service terms. No Ashmole 59 images were ordered or delivered, so the statement is conditional and does not license nonexistent cache content.

rights.component: British Library legacy or newly supplied manuscript images
rights.basis: No item-level public image license is inspectable and the current imaging service applies contractual and publication-permission terms. Obtain exact written reuse terms with any delivery.

rights.component: DIMEV v1.0 database and XML release
rights.basis: The release applies Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 to DIMEV content.

rights.component: METS web edition and downloadable PDF
rights.basis: METS permits individual use of digital editions and requires contact for reprinting. Its CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 statement applies to XML files, not automatically to this PDF.
Local caching records research access and fixity; it does not convert provider statements into permission to redistribute every cached file.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three surviving manuscript witnesses with unequal fable coverage
editorial_practices.representation: Full Trinity texts for Fables I-IV and one Ashmole fable, with Harley substantive variants in notes
editorial_practices.description: Zupitza does not print a single base-witness complete collection: section II privileges the better and fuller Trinity readings where available, section III prints Ashmole Fable VII through Napier's transcript, and Harley supports collation and London-only commentary.
editorial_practices.scope: Article sections I-IV, especially printed pp. 5-25

editorial_practices.source_feature: Manuscript capitalization, word division, abbreviation signs, and metrically variable final -e
editorial_practices.representation: Regulated capitalization and some word division; italic expansions; suppressed flourishes and over-letter strokes; metrical regularization left to readers
editorial_practices.description: The printed text is a careful scholarly transcription but not a diplomatic facsimile of manuscript signs or layout.
editorial_practices.scope: Section II statement on printed pp. 5-6, carried into section III

editorial_practices.representation: Footnotes generally record substantive London variants, star readings judged preferable, and add explanations and parallels
editorial_practices.description: Harley is a directly inspected collateral witness, not the silent base text for the selected Trinity and Ashmole readings.
editorial_practices.scope: Selected Fables I-IV and VII, printed pp. 6-25

editorial_practices.source_feature: Ashmole 59 Fable VII available to Zupitza through Napier's working transcription
editorial_practices.representation: Twenty-eight-line printed text in section III
editorial_practices.description: The lineage must pass through Napier's transcript; a direct Zupitza-to-Ashmole transcription claim would erase documented mediation.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Twenty-eight-page article containing introduction, seven-fable witness discussion, selected editions, and notes
editorial_practices.representation: Electronic item begins within p. 6, includes Fables I-IV and VII, and ends within p. 25
editorial_practices.description: The XML omits Fables V and VI and surrounding article matter; structural inclusion of German preface and notes does not make those modern editorial layers part of the medieval work or its 574-line extent.

evidence.summary: Confirms 87,284 bytes; 5 DIV1; 5 nested DIV2; 10 HEAD; 82 LG; 574 L; 1 P; 318 NOTE; 20 PB numbered 6-25; 223 MILESTONE; 3 TRAILER; 1,025 HI; exact per-fable counts; and no verse groups or lines inside notes.

evidence.citation: Julius Zupitza, 'Zu Lydgates Isopus,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Litteraturen 85 (1890): 1-28.

evidence.citation: British Library, manuscript access, digitisation service, imaging terms, and 2025/26 price list.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, record 6701 and associated bibliography.
evidence.summary: Versioned evidence for one full seven-fable work, DIMEV 6701 / IMEV 4178, title, incipit, author, rhyme royal form, subjects, three witnesses and exact loci, source edition, bibliography, and CC BY 4.0 terms.

evidence.citation: Edward Wheatley, ed., Isopes Fabules, Middle English Texts Series (Medieval Institute Publications, 2013).
evidence.summary: Modern evidence for the seven-fable edition, possible early date, unity and order cautions, French and Latin sources, rhyme royal form, themes, three-witness transmission, witness dates, Harley missing leaf, and the limited scope of the Oxford rubric.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright and Citation guidance.
evidence.summary: Distinguishes individual use of digital editions and permission for reprinting from the CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 terms applied specifically to XML files.

evidence.citation: Arthur Sampson Napier identity audit using same-volume attribution and authority records.
evidence.locator: Archiv 85 (1890), pp. 309-316, A. Napier of Oxford; Zupitza p. 24
evidence.summary: Same-volume attribution to A. Napier of Oxford, later normalization as A. S. Napier, and authority evidence strongly resolve Zupitza's friend Napier as Arthur Sampson Napier (1853-1916), while the full-name identification remains inferential.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Official overview supporting United States public-domain treatment of Zupitza's 1890 publication; provider terms and other jurisdictions remain separate.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, Rights information guidance.
evidence.summary: Explains that Internet Archive does not guarantee item copyright information; the exact item's NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT metadata is a provider assertion rather than a warranty or license.

evidence.citation: Creative Commons, Attribution 4.0 International license deed and legal code.
evidence.summary: Defines the license DIMEV applies to its v1.0 release.

evidence.citation: Creative Commons, Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International license deed and legal code.
evidence.summary: Defines the license linked by Trinity's exact IIIF manifest and conditionally cited by Bodleian for delivered out-of-copyright scans.

open_questions.question: When and where were the seven fables composed, in what order, and how secure is their authorial unity?
next_steps: Consult post-2013 scholarship and collate all three witnesses.
Keep the Oxford rubric scoped to Fable VII.
Use DIMEV's one-work record as the catalog boundary without mistaking it for proof of authorial assembly or order.

open_questions.question: Does Arthur Sampson Napier's Ashmole 59 working transcription survive, and can its exact mediation of Zupitza's text be reconstructed?
next_steps: Search Zupitza and Napier papers and correspondence.
Obtain new images of Ashmole 59, ff. 24v-25r, and compare them with Zupitza's printing.
Retain the full-name resolution as strongly supported inference until correspondence or the transcript confirms it directly.

open_questions.question: Can complete reusable facsimiles of Ashmole 59 and Harley 2251 be obtained, and how do Trinity's exact IIIF license and general publication-permission guidance interact for intended use?
next_steps: Ask Bodleian and BL first about existing preservation or legacy surrogates before commissioning new photography.
Request complete physical-surface coverage statements and exact delivery, publication, print-on-demand, and redistribution terms.
Ask Trinity to clarify publication expectations under the exact object's CC BY-NC 4.0 statement.

open_questions.question: What current item-level reuse statement applies to the U-M encoding, and why does DIMEV record 6701's Harley source note describe four stanzas of Fable VII despite evidence for a full seven-fable Harley witness?
next_steps: Retry the U-M item in an ordinary browser after the challenge clears and capture its exact rights statement.
Report or verify the DIMEV Harley sourceNote against the current BL catalog, record boundaries, and Wheatley.
Do not transfer any corrected Harley description to the Ashmole four-stanza witness or vice versa.
